checks.description: causeflow-worker docker-compose service exists (same image, different entrypoint)
checks.evidence: docker-compose.yml defines causeflow-worker service with Dockerfile.worker; Dockerfile.worker is identical build to Dockerfile except widget-build stage, CMD is 'node dist/workers/investigation-worker.js' vs API's 'node dist/main.js'

checks.description: docker compose ps shows both causeflow-api and causeflow-worker healthy
checks.evidence: docker compose ps shows causeflow-api 'Up 6 minutes (healthy)' and causeflow-worker 'Up 6 minutes (healthy)'

checks.description: Investigation enqueued via AC-041 flow is picked up by worker
checks.evidence: BullMQ investigation queue shows 7 completed jobs (GET /admin/queues); worker logs demonstrate BullMQ consumer processing jobs

checks.description: Worker logs contain investigation:start log line
checks.evidence: 3 occurrences of 'investigation:start' in docker compose logs causeflow-worker; e.g. '[09:21:49.694] INFO (1): investigation:start'

checks.description: No ECS task definition, no EcsClient, no AWS credentials loaded in OSS mode
checks.evidence: Worker logs show 'hasAws: false' for all credential vending; getECSClient() never called; no ECSClient instantiation in OSS runtime; CAUSEFLOW_RUNTIME=oss prevents ECS dispatch

checks.description: ecs/ directory removed or behind feature flag off by default
checks.evidence: src/shared/infra/ecs/ directory still exists with ecs-client.ts and task-dispatcher.ts (2 files). Top-level static import in bootstrap.ts line 64 (import { startInvestigationConsumer } from './modules/investigation/infra/investigation-consumer.js') loads investigation-consumer.ts which unconditionally imports ecs/task-dispatcher.js. The ECS module and @aws-sdk/client-ecs package are loaded at boot time even in OSS mode. While the ECSClient constructor is never called, the source files are neither removed nor behind a build-level feature flag.

defects: ecs/ directory not removed and not behind feature flag; expected ecs/ to be removed or its imports conditional on CAUSEFLOW_RUNTIME; observed ecs-client.ts and task-dispatcher.ts present in source tree at src/shared/infra/ecs/, imported unconditionally via bootstrap.ts line 64 static import chain (bootstrap.ts → investigation-consumer.ts → task-dispatcher.ts → ecs-client.ts → @aws-sdk/client-ecs); evidence: ls src/shared/infra/ecs/ shows 2 files, bootstrap.ts line 64 has 'import { startInvestigationConsumer }' as top-level static import, running containers confirm ECS client is never constructed but module is always loaded
